pitlaneR 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

Getting Started with pitlaneR

Source: vignettes/pitlaneR.Rmd
pitlaneR.Rmd

pitlaneR provides tidy access to Formula 1 data from the Jolpica API, a free, community-maintained successor to the Ergast API. It covers the entire history of the F1 World Championship from 1950 to the present — no API key or Python dependency required.

Important: the current version of this R package was largely produce by Claude, as part of Posit’s AI beta. This code is only partially tested, and so should be treated with caution. Read about this process on my blog.

All functions return tibbles with snake_case column names and automatic type conversion. API responses are cached in memory for one hour, so repeated calls are fast.

library(pitlaneR)

Seasons

f1_seasons() returns every F1 season available in the database.

f1_seasons()

Race schedule

f1_races() returns the race calendar for a given season, including circuit details and session times. It defaults to the current year.

# Current season
f1_races()

# A specific season
f1_races(2024)

Circuits

f1_circuits() returns circuit information, including geographic coordinates. Pass a season to see only circuits used that year, or omit it for all circuits in the database.

# Circuits used in 2024
f1_circuits(2024)

# All circuits ever used in F1
f1_circuits()

Drivers and constructors

f1_drivers() and f1_constructors() return reference information about drivers and teams. Both accept an optional season to filter to participants from that year.

# Drivers who raced in 2024
f1_drivers(2024)

# All constructors in the database
f1_constructors()

Race results

f1_results() returns finishing results for a season, or for a specific round within a season. Each row represents one driver’s result for one race.

# All results for the 2024 season
f1_results(2024)

# Just round 1
f1_results(2024, round = 1)

The returned tibble includes grid position, finishing position, points, status, driver and constructor details, and (where available) fastest lap information.

Qualifying results

f1_qualifying() returns qualifying session results, including Q1, Q2, and Q3 lap times.

Note: Qualifying data is only available from the 1994 season onward. Requesting an earlier season will raise an error.

f1_qualifying(2024, round = 1)

Sprint results

f1_sprint() returns results from sprint races, which were introduced in the 2021 season. The function returns an empty tibble for seasons or rounds without sprints.

# All sprint results from 2024
f1_sprint(2024)

Championship standings

f1_driver_standings() and f1_constructor_standings() return the championship table for a given season. By default they return the final (or latest available) standings; pass a round number to see the standings at a specific point in the season.

# Final 2024 drivers' championship
f1_driver_standings(2024)

# Constructors' standings after round 10
f1_constructor_standings(2024, round = 10)

Lap times

f1_laps() returns lap-by-lap timing data for a specific race. Both season and round are required. You can optionally request a single lap.

Note: Lap time data is available from the 1996 season onward. This endpoint can return a large volume of data; a progress bar is shown for multi-page fetches.

# All laps from the 2024 Bahrain GP (round 1)
f1_laps(2024, round = 1)

# Just the first lap
f1_laps(2024, round = 1, lap = 1)

Pit stops

f1_pit_stops() returns pit stop data for a specific race, including the stop number, lap, time of day, and duration.

Note: Pit stop data is available from the 2012 season onward.

f1_pit_stops(2024, round = 1)

Caching

API responses are cached in memory for up to one hour to avoid redundant requests. If you need fresh data (e.g. during a live race weekend), clear the cache:

f1_clear_cache()

Summary of functions

Function Description Data availability
f1_seasons() All F1 seasons 1950–present
f1_races() Race schedule and session times 1950–present
f1_circuits() Circuit information and coordinates 1950–present
f1_drivers() Driver information 1950–present
f1_constructors() Constructor (team) information 1950–present
f1_results() Race finishing results 1950–present
f1_qualifying() Qualifying session results 1994–present
f1_sprint() Sprint race results 2021–present
f1_driver_standings() Drivers’ championship standings 1950–present
f1_constructor_standings() Constructors’ championship standings 1958–present
f1_laps() Lap-by-lap timing data 1996–present
f1_pit_stops() Pit stop data 2012–present
f1_clear_cache() Clear cached API responses —
